content.text: Retrieval Source Provenance for RAG Pipelines

A retrieval-augmented generation pipeline injects knowledge into the model's context from an external document store. Every chunk that reaches the model carries implicit attribution to the document it came from. Source provenance makes that attribution explicit and queryable: which document, which version, which section, retrieved by which query, at what similarity score, and subject to which sensitivity label. Without provenance, the RAG pipeline is a black box that cannot support citations to users, explain why a document deletion affects past responses, or demonstrate to an auditor that RESTRICTED documents were never retrieved for users without the appropriate clearance.

This topic covers the provenance data model for documents and chunks, the metadata that must be stored at each pipeline stage, sensitivity-label gating at retrieval time, citation generation, and the query patterns used for retraction analysis and audit.

Provenance begins at ingestion. When a document enters the knowledge base it receives a stable identifier and a snapshot of its identity at that moment. If the document is updated, a new version record is created rather than overwriting the old one — the old chunks remain in the vector store with their original provenance until explicitly decommissioned.

from __future__ import annotations
import hashlib, json, time, uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

class DocStatus(str, Enum):
    ACTIVE       = "active"
    SUPERSEDED   = "superseded"   # newer version exists
    RETRACTED    = "retracted"    # must not be served to any user

@dataclass
class DocumentProvenanceRecord:
    """Created once per document version ingested into the knowledge base."""
    doc_id:           str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    source_uri:       str = ""          # s3://bucket/path or https://... or file://
    source_title:     str = ""
    source_version:   str = ""          # e.g. git SHA, file hash, or semantic version
    content_hash:     str = ""          # SHA-256 of the raw document bytes
    sensitivity_label: str = "INTERNAL"
    owner_team:       str = ""
    ingested_utc:     float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    status:           DocStatus = DocStatus.ACTIVE
    superseded_by:    Optional[str] = None  # doc_id of the newer version
    retraction_reason: Optional[str] = None

    @classmethod
    def from_bytes(cls, content: bytes, source_uri: str, source_title: str,
                   source_version: str, label: str, owner: str) -> "DocumentProvenanceRecord":
        return cls(
            source_uri=source_uri,
            source_title=source_title,
            source_version=source_version,
            content_hash=hashlib.sha256(content).hexdigest(),
            sensitivity_label=label,
            owner_team=owner,
        )

    def retract(self, reason: str) -> None:
        object.__setattr__(self, "status", DocStatus.RETRACTED)
        object.__setattr__(self, "retraction_reason", reason)

Each chunk produced by the chunking stage inherits its parent document's identity and adds its own position metadata. The chunk identifier embeds the document ID so that a chunk reference always resolves to its parent without a secondary lookup.

from __future__ import annotations
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
import time

@dataclass
class ChunkProvenanceRecord:
    """Created once per chunk during document processing."""
    chunk_id:         str           # f"{doc_id}::{chunk_index:04d}"
    doc_id:           str
    chunk_index:      int
    char_start:       int           # byte offset in the original document
    char_end:         int
    content_hash:     str           # SHA-256 of the chunk text
    sensitivity_label: str          # inherited from DocumentProvenanceRecord
    embedding_model:  str           # e.g. "text-embedding-3-large"
    embedding_dim:    int
    vector_collection: str          # vector store collection name
    indexed_utc:      float = field(default_factory=time.time)

    @classmethod
    def make_chunk_id(cls, doc_id: str, index: int) -> str:
        return f"{doc_id}::{index:04d}"

    def parent_doc_id(self) -> str:
        return self.chunk_id.split("::")[0]

The retrieval stage is the enforcement point for access control on RAG sources. Before returning a retrieved chunk to the prompt builder, the retriever checks the chunk's sensitivity label against the requesting user's clearance level. A user with INTERNAL clearance must not receive chunks from CONFIDENTIAL or RESTRICTED documents, even if those chunks have high retrieval similarity scores.

from __future__ import annotations
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional
from chunk_registry import ChunkProvenanceRecord, ChunkRegistry
from doc_registry import DocumentProvenanceRecord, DocRegistry, DocStatus

LABEL_RANK: dict[str, int] = {
    "PUBLIC": 0, "INTERNAL": 1, "RESTRICTED": 2,
    "CONFIDENTIAL": 3, "REGULATED": 4,
}

@dataclass
class RetrievedChunk:
    chunk_id:      str
    doc_id:        str
    text:          str
    score:         float          # cosine similarity from vector store
    sensitivity_label: str
    source_title:  str
    source_version: str
    source_uri:    str
    char_start:    int
    char_end:      int

class ProvenanceAwareRetriever:
    def __init__(
        self,
        vector_store,             # any client with .query(collection, embedding, top_k)
        chunk_registry: "ChunkRegistry",
        doc_registry:   "DocRegistry",
    ):
        self.vs  = vector_store
        self.cr  = chunk_registry
        self.dr  = doc_registry

    def retrieve(
        self,
        query_embedding: list[float],
        collection: str,
        top_k: int,
        user_label: str,
        lineage_id: str,
    ) -> list[RetrievedChunk]:
        raw = self.vs.query(collection, query_embedding, top_k=top_k * 3)  # over-fetch

        results: list[RetrievedChunk] = []
        for hit in raw:
            chunk_id = hit["id"]
            score    = hit["score"]

            chunk = self.cr.get(chunk_id)
            if chunk is None:
                continue  # orphan — chunk not in registry

            doc = self.dr.get(chunk.doc_id)
            if doc is None or doc.status == DocStatus.RETRACTED:
                continue  # document retracted — never serve

            # Sensitivity gate
            if LABEL_RANK.get(chunk.sensitivity_label, 99) > LABEL_RANK.get(user_label, 0):
                continue  # user clearance insufficient

            results.append(RetrievedChunk(
                chunk_id=chunk_id,
                doc_id=chunk.doc_id,
                text=hit["text"],
                score=score,
                sensitivity_label=chunk.sensitivity_label,
                source_title=doc.source_title,
                source_version=doc.source_version,
                source_uri=doc.source_uri,
                char_start=chunk.char_start,
                char_end=chunk.char_end,
            ))
            if len(results) == top_k:
                break

        # Log retrieval event for provenance lineage linkage
        self._log_retrieval(lineage_id, results)
        return results

    def _log_retrieval(self, lineage_id: str, chunks: list[RetrievedChunk]) -> None:
        # Append to retrieval_log.ndjson (not shown) — links lineage_id to chunk_ids
        pass

The retrieval log is the join table between the lineage store (from the previous topic) and the chunk provenance registry. Each retrieval event records which chunks were considered (pre-filter) and which were served (post-filter), so that a later audit can prove that a RESTRICTED chunk was correctly blocked for a user with only INTERNAL clearance.

Enterprise Gen AI applications are expected to show citations: references that tell the user which source document each part of the answer came from. Citations serve both UX (users can verify claims) and compliance (the audit trail shows exactly which documents contributed to a response). The citation object is built from the chunk's provenance record after retrieval.

from __future__ import annotations
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional

@dataclass
class Citation:
    source_title:   str
    source_uri:     str
    source_version: str
    chunk_id:       str
    char_start:     int
    char_end:       int
    score:          float           # retrieval similarity (0–1)
    sensitivity_label: str

    def display_text(self) -> str:
        """Human-readable citation for chat UI footnotes."""
        score_pct = round(self.score * 100, 1)
        return (
            f"{self.source_title} "
            f"(v{self.source_version}, chars {self.char_start}–{self.char_end}, "
            f"relevance {score_pct}%)"
        )

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "title":    self.source_title,
            "uri":      self.source_uri,
            "version":  self.source_version,
            "chunk_id": self.chunk_id,
            "location": {"start": self.char_start, "end": self.char_end},
            "score":    self.score,
            "label":    self.sensitivity_label,
        }

    @classmethod
    def from_retrieved_chunk(cls, chunk: "RetrievedChunk") -> "Citation":
        return cls(
            source_title=chunk.source_title,
            source_uri=chunk.source_uri,
            source_version=chunk.source_version,
            chunk_id=chunk.chunk_id,
            char_start=chunk.char_start,
            char_end=chunk.char_end,
            score=chunk.score,
            sensitivity_label=chunk.sensitivity_label,
        )

When a document must be retracted — due to an error, a regulatory hold, a DSAR erasure request, or reclassification — the retraction has two parts: removing the document's chunks from the vector store so they cannot be retrieved in future requests, and identifying all past responses that were generated while those chunks were active. The lineage store handles the second part; the chunk registry handles the first.

from __future__ import annotations
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from chunk_registry import ChunkRegistry
from doc_registry import DocRegistry, DocStatus
from retrieval_log import RetrievalLog

@dataclass
class RetractionResult:
    doc_id:            str
    chunks_deindexed:  int
    affected_lineage_ids: list[str]   # responses that used these chunks
    affected_user_ids:    list[str]

def retract_document(
    doc_id: str,
    reason: str,
    doc_registry: DocRegistry,
    chunk_registry: ChunkRegistry,
    vector_store,
    retrieval_log: RetrievalLog,
) -> RetractionResult:
    # 1. Mark document as retracted in registry
    doc = doc_registry.get(doc_id)
    if doc is None:
        raise ValueError(f"Document {doc_id} not found")
    doc_registry.retract(doc_id, reason)

    # 2. Remove all chunks from the vector store
    chunks = chunk_registry.list_chunks_for_doc(doc_id)
    for chunk in chunks:
        vector_store.delete(chunk.vector_collection, chunk.chunk_id)

    # 3. Find all past lineage records that used any of these chunks
    affected_lineage: list[str] = []
    affected_users:   list[str] = []
    for chunk in chunks:
        for event in retrieval_log.find_by_chunk(chunk.chunk_id):
            if event.lineage_id not in affected_lineage:
                affected_lineage.append(event.lineage_id)

    return RetractionResult(
        doc_id=doc_id,
        chunks_deindexed=len(chunks),
        affected_lineage_ids=affected_lineage,
        affected_user_ids=affected_users,
    )
Provenance Metadata Schema for Vector Stores

Vector stores that support metadata filtering (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, ChromaDB, Azure AI Search) should carry the full provenance context as searchable metadata on each vector. This enables the retriever to filter by sensitivity label, document status, and version directly at the vector store layer — before the results return to the application.
Content hash of the document at ingestion
Access control gate at retrieval time
Unix timestamp of embedding; used for freshness decay
Model used to produce this vector

# Example: upsert a chunk into Qdrant with full provenance metadata
from qdrant_client import QdrantClient
from qdrant_client.models import PointStruct

def upsert_chunk_with_provenance(
    client: QdrantClient,
    collection: str,
    chunk: "ChunkProvenanceRecord",
    embedding: list[float],
    chunk_text: str,
    doc: "DocumentProvenanceRecord",
) -> None:
    point = PointStruct(
        id=chunk.chunk_id,
        vector=embedding,
        payload={
            "text":              chunk_text,
            "chunk_id":          chunk.chunk_id,
            "doc_id":            chunk.doc_id,
            "doc_version":       doc.source_version,
            "sensitivity_label": chunk.sensitivity_label,
            "doc_status":        doc.status.value,
            "owner_team":        doc.owner_team,
            "source_title":      doc.source_title,
            "source_uri":        doc.source_uri,
            "char_start":        chunk.char_start,
            "char_end":          chunk.char_end,
            "indexed_utc":       chunk.indexed_utc,
            "embedding_model":   chunk.embedding_model,
        },
    )
    client.upsert(collection_name=collection, points=[point])
Three common gaps weaken RAG provenance in production deployments:
Retraction cannot identify affected chunks; retrieval log orphan entries
Re-ingest all documents through provenance-aware pipeline; reject chunks without metadata
Sensitivity label not propagated to vector store
Label gating bypassed if application layer is skipped; label in retrieval log does not match vector store
Embed label in vector metadata at index time; validate at retrieval with server-side filter
Retrieval log not linked to lineage store
Cannot answer 'which responses used document X'; DSAR and retraction impact analysis fail
Write lineage_id into every retrieval event; test round-trip query before go-live
Old chunks remain active in vector store under new doc_id; retraction of old doc_id misses them
Treat every new version as a new document; supersede old doc_id explicitly; deindex old chunks

Retrieval source provenance tracks the identity of every document and chunk that enters the RAG pipeline and connects it to the inference events it influenced. The data model has three layers: the document provenance record (created at ingestion, versioned on update, status-flagged on retraction), the chunk provenance record (inheriting the document's label, carrying position metadata), and the retrieval event log (linking chunk IDs to lineage IDs from the inference pipeline). Sensitivity label gating at retrieval time enforces access control before chunks reach the prompt. Citation generation converts the provenance record into user-facing footnotes. Document retraction combines the chunk registry, vector store deletion, and retrieval log to identify and communicate the impact on past responses — the primary justification for the entire provenance infrastructure.
